ChangeRequest.name: Add security identifier type CUSIP/SEDOL
ChangeRequest.comment:
<!-- See: documentation on [Issues in GitLab](https://gitlab.sofa.dev/sdd-bird-team/bird-logical-data-model/-/wikis/Issues-in-GitLab) -->
# Overview Securities are identified by ISIN. There are also other types of identifiers for securities in use. This request is to add both `Security identifier type` and its corresponding security identifier to the LDM
### Contact person _[me@ecb.europa.eu]_
### Business definition paragraph 5.4.1 Security identifier code of SHS guideline (see below)
#### Description > A standard code, agreed with the relevant NCB, which uniquely identifies the security and information on the type of code used to uniquely identify the security. Additional clarifications
> This is a security identification number of a **non-ISIN instrument**, which should ensure the unique and time-consistent identification of the security.
> Moreover, the type of identifier used for the non-ISIN security should be provided as a separate variable, e.g. to specify that the Committee on Uniform Securities Identification Procedures (CUSIP) code has been used as an identification number for the non-ISIN instrument.
### Reason for inclusion Needed for IReF and for SHS
### Related objects - _Security and exchange tradable derivative_ - _Security_ - _Exchange tradable derivative_ - _International securities identification number (ISIN) security_
### Relevant Framework/Template - SHS https://www.ecb.europa.eu/pub/pdf/other/ecb.guidance_notes_to_reporting_agents_on_shs_regulation202005~f7bce14823.en.pdf - IReF https://sdd.ecb.de/bycode/variable/ECB30/SCRTY_ID_TYP (ECB internal link)
### Before closing checklist - [ ] Assignee for review - [ ] Assignee to close - [ ] Other actions
